EU Cybersecurity Certification Scheme for ICT products based on Common Criteria
Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information — France's national cybersecurity authority
Cryptography Research and Evaluation Committees — Japan's government body evaluating cryptographic techniques for public and government use
Korea Internet & Security Agency and National Intelligence Service — South Korea's cybersecurity and cryptography standardization authorities
Office of State Commercial Cryptography Administration (国家密码管理局) — China's regulatory authority for commercial cryptography
European Telecommunications Standards Institute — global standards body for telecoms PQC including quantum-safe algorithm suites for 5G/6G
German Federal Office for Information Security — Europe's most detailed national PQC technical guidance authority
US National Security Agency — mandates PQC for all national security systems via CNSA 2.0 with the most aggressive government timeline
Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification — US DoD supply chain cybersecurity certification requirement
Federal Information Security Modernization Act — mandates information security programs for all US federal agencies
EU regulation establishing cybersecurity requirements for connected products throughout their lifecycle
India National Quantum Mission post-quantum cryptography migration roadmap for all sectors
Hong Kong Monetary Authority quantum preparedness framework for financial institutions
UAE Cybersecurity Council national encryption policy mandating PQC transition
CISA product category list and federal procurement guidance for PQC per EO 14306
G7 Cyber Expert Group coordinated PQC transition roadmap for the financial sector
OASIS PKCS#11 v3.2 standard adding ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA mechanisms for hardware security modules
CA/Browser Forum ballot enabling ML-DSA PQC signatures in S/MIME certificates
NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol for PQC algorithm testing
Taiwan Ministry of Digital Affairs five-year PQC implementation plan
Malaysia NACSA national PQC migration roadmap — first ASEAN nation with comprehensive plan
Saudi Arabia National Cybersecurity Authority essential cybersecurity controls with expanded cryptography domain
Bank for International Settlements quantum-readiness roadmap for global financial systems
3GPP SA3 study on integrating PQC into 5G security protocols
Trusted Computing Group TPM 2.0 library specification update incorporating PQC algorithms
NIST NCCoE practical enterprise PQC migration guide with reference architectures
NIST algorithm deprecation roadmap — SHA-1 and weak RSA/ECC disallowed after 2030
India CERT-In expanded Bill of Materials requirements including Cryptographic BOM and Quantum Readiness BOM
Italy Agenzia per la Cybersicurezza Nazionale post-quantum cryptography guidance
Spain Centro Criptologico Nacional four-phase PQC transition approach
Bahrain National Cyber Security Centre quantum-safe cybersecurity deployment across government
Central Bank of Jordan sector-wide roadmap for transitioning banks to quantum-resistant encryption
UK Cross Market Operational Resilience Group PQC guidance for financial sector
Europol QSFF call to action for financial sector PQC migration

Accredited Standards Committee X9 develops cryptographic standards for the financial services industry. X9 Financial PKI (launched June 2025, with DigiCert) provides PQC-enabled certificate infrastructure for banks, payment terminals, and financial enterprises. Key PQC standards: X9.146 (Quantum TLS for financial services), X9 TR-50 (Quantum Techniques in CMS), and the X9 PQC Financial Readiness Needs Assessment (2025).
Industry consortium coordinating PQC readiness for PKI operators and certificate authorities
Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Working Group developing quantum security controls for cloud environments
ITU Telecommunication Standardization Sector Study Group 17 on security standards for global telecom PQC transition
NIST recommendation for stateful hash-based signature schemes (LMS and XMSS)
International standard for security requirements for cryptographic modules — equivalent of FIPS 140-3
ETSI Group Specification series for Quantum Key Distribution covering protocols interfaces and network management
French national cybersecurity certification scheme — issued world first PQC product certifications in 2025
Japanese Cryptographic Module Validation Program — Japan equivalent of NIST CMVP for government procurement
Singapore Cyber Security Agency national guidelines for quantum-safe transition across all sectors
Brazilian data protection authority ANPD guidance on cryptographic requirements under LGPD with PQC implications
Danish Centre for Cyber Security post-quantum cryptography guidance for government and critical infrastructure
New York Department of Financial Services cybersecurity regulation with explicit encryption and key management requirements
ETSI baseline cybersecurity standard for consumer IoT products with cryptographic requirements affecting long-lived devices
Post-Quantum Cryptography Alliance — Linux Foundation project advancing PQC adoption through open-source tooling and community collaboration
PQC Coalition — MITRE-led cooperative advancing quantum-safe migration across critical infrastructure sectors
Quantum Economic Development Consortium — NIST-established consortium enabling the quantum ecosystem including PQC readiness assessment

APRA Prudential Standard CPS 234 Information Security — mandatory for all APRA-regulated entities including banks, insurers, and superannuation funds. Requires robust information security capabilities proportionate to information asset criticality; cryptographic controls are explicitly in scope.

Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) Essential Eight Maturity Model — eight baseline mitigation strategies mandated for Australian government entities and widely adopted in critical infrastructure and finance. Patch management and application hardening strategies directly intersect PQC transition planning.

Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) with Australian Privacy Principles (APPs) — enforced by the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC). APP 11 requires organisations to take reasonable steps to protect personal information, including cryptographic security controls. Post-quantum transition affects long-term data confidentiality.

Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Act 2006 — enforced by AUSTRAC. Regulated reporting entities (banks, fintechs, crypto exchanges) must maintain secure cryptographic systems for transaction reporting and record-keeping. AML/CTF Rules require integrity and authenticity controls over financial intelligence data.

Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions guideline B-13 on operational risk and resilience. Covers cryptographic controls for federally regulated financial institutions (FRFIs). Institutions must maintain crypto inventories, assess quantum risk, and implement crypto-agility.

Canadian quantum-readiness best practices and guidelines published by the Centre for Financial Democracy and Innovation Regulation (CFDIR) in partnership with Innovation Science and Economic Development Canada. Covers quantum threat assessment, migration prioritization, and hybrid PQC deployment for financial sector.

UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology perspectives on PQC transition planning from Critical National Infrastructure sectors. Sets expectations for CNI operators (energy, water, transport, finance) to engage with NCSC migration timelines and report readiness.
Autorité de Contrôle Prudentiel et de Résolution — French prudential regulator for banking and insurance sectors, supervised by Banque de France
Autorité des marchés financiers — French securities and financial markets regulator
Australian Defence Security Principles Framework — cryptography transition requirements for all ADF personnel and cleared contractors
South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act — data protection regulation with strong encryption and breach-notification requirements relevant to PQC migration for long-retention data
Nigeria Data Protection Regulation + Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 — jointly enforced by the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC), with explicit encryption obligations for personal data
Kenya Data Protection Act — enforced by the Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC); imposes encryption-at-rest and in-transit obligations on data controllers
Egypt Law No. 151 of 2020 on the Protection of Personal Data — enforced by the Egyptian Data Protection Center (DPC); mandates encryption and security controls for personal data processing
African Union Convention on Cyber Security and Personal Data Protection — pan-continental instrument harmonising cybersecurity and data-protection regimes across AU member states
EU Cybersecurity Certification Scheme Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms v2.0

BSI ongoing PQC pre-standardization activity covering algorithm selection commentary, harmonization with NIST FIPS 203/204/205/206, and German-context migration recommendations. Advisory / forward-looking; tracks closely with NIST PQC programme on its own timeline.

UK PRA supervisory statement on operational resilience, scoped to Tier 1/2 banks, insurers, and major investment firms. The most specific UK supervisory channel on quantum-risk timelines for financial firms.

UK Information Commissioner''s Office guidance on encryption as a Reasonable Step under UK GDPR Article 32. Codifies current data-at-rest and data-in-transit cryptographic-adequacy expectations; does not mandate PQC.

Norwegian National Security Authority's foundational ICT-security principles — closest Nordic analogue to NIST CSF. NSM publishes explicit PQC positioning and is the unambiguous primary Nordic crypto-guidance authority.

Swedish Civil Contingencies Agency cyber-resilience guidance. MSB is the civil-contingency umbrella; for algorithm-level guidance, cross-reference MSB Metodstöd documents and NCSC-SE bulletins (which sit across SÄPO/MSB depending on topic).

Finnish National Cyber Security Centre (under Traficom). 'Kyberturvallisuuden tilannekuva' situational picture and technical bulletins drive PQC awareness and timing across Finnish public and critical-infrastructure sectors.

Chile Agencia Nacional de Ciberseguridad (established by Ley 21.663 / 2023) is the designated national cybersecurity authority. Has already issued binding technical standards across critical-information sectors.

Mexican National Banking and Securities Commission cryptographic-control expectations for banks under the Circular Única de Bancos (CUB). Mature financial-sector cyber-regulation channel — preferred over fragmented NOM cyber standards.
Argentina Oficina Nacional de Tecnologías de Información — the correct cyber authority for federal government (AFIP is the tax authority; BCRA covers financial sector — both are separate from ONTI).

Brazilian central bank Resolution 4.893 sets encryption requirements for financial sector institutions. Pairs with ANPD (LGPD) on data-protection cryptographic adequacy; both are well-documented and more mature than most LATAM cyber-regulator entries.

Indonesia Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection — establishes a national data-protection regime with explicit obligations on technical and organisational security measures including encryption.
Thailand PDPA B.E. 2562 (2019) — comprehensive data-protection law enforced by the PDPC; requires appropriate security measures for personal data including encryption.

Vietnam Law on Cybersecurity (2018) plus Decree 13/2023 on Personal Data Protection — joint framework enforced by MIC and the Ministry of Public Security (A05); mandates encryption for personal data processing and cross-border transfers.
Philippines Data Privacy Act of 2012 (Republic Act No. 10173) — enforced by the National Privacy Commission (NPC); mandates encryption for sensitive personal information at rest and in transit.

Estonian Cybersecurity Act (2018, updated for NIS2 transposition 2024) — enforced by RIA (Information System Authority); applies to operators of essential and important services; aligns with EUCC and ENISA PQC guidance.
Irish National Cyber Security Centre PQC guidance plus NIS2 transposition via the National Cyber Security Bill 2024 — enforced by NCSC-IE under DECC; aligns with ENISA PQC guidelines and EUCC.

Belgian NIS2 transposition (Law of 26 April 2024) + the Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB) Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 — covers essential and important entities with explicit reference to post-quantum migration.

Austrian NIS2 transposition (NISG 2024) + A-SIT (Zentrum für sichere Informationstechnologie - Austria) guidance — covers operators of essential and important services; A-SIT is the recognised national crypto-evaluation body.

Polish National Cybersecurity System Act (2018, NIS2 amendment 2024) — enforced by Ministry of Digital Affairs with NASK as the national CERT/research body; tracks NIST PQC for public-sector adoption.
Colombia Statutory Law 1581 of 2012 (Habeas Data) plus Decree 1377/2013 — enforced by SIC (Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio); requires adequate technical measures for personal-data security.
Peru Law No. 29733 on Personal Data Protection (2011) + Supreme Decree 003-2013-JUS — enforced by ANPD; requires technical security measures including encryption for sensitive personal data.
Uruguay Law 18.331 on Protection of Personal Data + URCDP guidance — enforced by the Personal Data Regulation and Control Unit (URCDP) within AGESIC; Uruguay holds an EU adequacy decision since 2012.

Russian national cryptographic standards maintained by Technical Committee 26 (TC 26) of Rosstandart under FSB oversight — GOST R 34.10-2012 (signatures), GOST R 34.11-2012 (Streebog), GOST R 34.12-2015 (Kuznyechik/Magma).
Turkey Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 (KVKK, 2016) — enforced by the Personal Data Protection Authority (KVK Kurumu); requires technical security measures including encryption.
Advisory Circular describing an acceptable means for obtaining operational authorization for an aircraft certified with a special condition related to the security of the onboard computer network.
A technical report studying the migration of Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) and Cooperative ITS (C-ITS) to Quantum-Safe Cryptography, applying the methodology of ETSI TR 103 619.
The provided document text consists of raw PDF binary data and object streams, containing no readable regulatory text regarding post-quantum cryptography or automotive standards.
Part 1 of the Common Criteria provides the introduction and general model for the security evaluation of IT products, defining the framework for assessing security claims.

CISA's initiative to unify efforts with interagency and industry partners to address threats posed by quantum computing and support the transition to post-quantum cryptography for critical infrastructure and government networks.

The document outlines the regulatory framework for commercial cryptography in China, including the revised 'Regulations on the Administration of Commercial Cryptography' and specific management provisions for cryptographic use in critical information infrastructure.
The BSI issues security certificates for IT products based on Common Criteria (CC) or ITSEC, involving technical testing by accredited laboratories and evaluation by the certification body.
Technical specification defining efficient quantum-safe hybrid key exchanges with hidden access policies, including Hybrid Traceable KEMAC and NIKE-based KEMs.

Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 establishes harmonised rules for the cybersecurity of products with digital elements, requiring manufacturers to ensure security by design and by default, including protection against cryptographic vulnerabilities.
Regulation (EU) 2024/1062 on horizontal cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements, mandating security-by-design and lifecycle management.
Mandates that in-scope financial firms identify important business services, set impact tolerances, and ensure they can operate within those tolerances during disruptions.

Mandatory directions issued by CERT-In under Section 70B of the IT Act 2000 requiring entities to synchronize clocks, report cyber incidents within 6 hours, maintain logs for 180 days, and register specific data for cloud/VPN providers.

Japan Information Technology Security Evaluation and Certification Scheme (JISEC) is a scheme under which third-party evaluation facilities evaluate whether IT product security is effective and reliably implemented in accordance with ISO/IEC 15408.
Risk management principles and best practice standards to guide financial institutions in managing technology risk.

The OCC issues guidance requiring banking organizations to assess and manage cybersecurity risks associated with the transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), including inventorying cryptographic assets and planning for migration.

A regulatory framework issued by the Reserve Bank of India mandating scheduled commercial banks to implement a robust cyber security policy, establish Security Operations Centres, and enhance resilience against cyber threats.

Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 establishes horizontal cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements placed on the EU market, mandating risk management, vulnerability handling, and security updates throughout the product lifecycle.
The provided text is an error page indicating the requested content was not found, containing only generic site navigation and footer information.
The document text provided consists of raw PDF binary data and compressed streams, containing no readable regulatory text, mandates, or PQC requirements.

A NERC Reliability Standard mandating the management of electronic access to Bulk Electric System (BES) Cyber Systems by specifying controlled Electronic Security Perimeters to protect against compromise leading to misoperation or instability.
South African legislation regulating the processing of personal information, establishing conditions for lawful processing, data subject rights, and security safeguards.
